items.title: Name tests as full sentences describing the behaviour
items.why: A failing test should tell you what's broken from the name alone.

items.title: One behaviour per test
items.prefer: Five short tests, each focused on one observable behaviour.
items.why: When a giant test fails, only the first assertion's failure is shown; the rest is hidden.

items.title: Parametrize tables of inputs
items.avoid: Copy-pasting the same test body for 5 different inputs.
items.why: Less duplication, clearer intent, each parameter shows as a separate test result.

items.title: Keep tests fast — milliseconds, not seconds
items.avoid: Network calls, real time.sleep, real DB writes in unit tests.
items.prefer: Mock external dependencies; use `freezegun` for time; use in-memory fakes (SQLite :memory:) when appropriate.
items.why: Slow tests stop getting run; fast tests run on every save.
items.example: Aim for a full unit suite under 30 seconds in CI.

items.title: Add a regression test for every bug fix
items.avoid: Fixing a bug without writing a test that would have caught it.
items.prefer: Reproduce the bug in a failing test FIRST, then make it pass with the fix.
items.why: Otherwise the bug returns within a few sprints when someone refactors.
items.example: Comment the test with the bug ID: `# regression test for #421`.
